ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT does not have a specific code for carbonated water; closest parent is Water (substance) 11713004.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of soda water is approximately 330 ml (one can or small bottle), equivalent to 330 g, as commonly sold in Singapore hawker centres and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated water, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174831)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for plain carbonated water (club soda / soda water) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values are per 100 g of beverage.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; plain carbonated water contains no glycemic carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain soda water contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and therefore has no measurable glycemic response. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) per Project Veritas zero-calorie beverage convention. GL per 330 g serving = 0.0. University of Sydney GI Database confirms that non-caloric carbonated waters do not raise blood glucose.

hypertension.guidance: Plain soda water contains approximately 10 mg sodium per 100 ml, contributing roughly 33 mg per 330 ml serving, which is negligible relative to the 2000 mg daily sodium limit recommended for hypertensive individuals and poses no clinically meaningful concern.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Soda water contains no sugar or digestible carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it a safe and recommended hydration choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Absence of fat, cholesterol, and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Soda water is free of saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and added sugars, and its negligible sodium content means it does not adversely affect cardiovascular risk factors when consumed as a substitute for sugary beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soda water (苏打水) is plain water that has been carbonated by dissolving carbon dioxide gas under pressure, producing a clear, effervescent, calorie-free beverage. It contains no sugar, no fat, no protein, and no alcohol. The only nutritionally notable component is a small amount of sodium (approximately 10 mg per 100 ml) and trace minerals such as potassium and calcium, which vary by brand. Soda water is widely consumed in Singapore as a standalone drink, as a mixer with cordials or fruit juices, and as a palate cleanser alongside hawker meals. It is sold in cans and bottles at hawker centres, coffee shops (kopitiams), and convenience stores. Clinically, soda water is an excellent hydration choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease because it provides hydration without contributing calories, sugar, or significant sodium. It should not be confused with tonic water (which contains quinine and added sugar) or flavoured sparkling water (which may contain added sugars or artificial sweeteners). The food_id GD003405 was sourced from field survey data, listed under Hock Kee Braised Beef Noodles 福记红烧牛肉面 at Lorong Telok, priced at SGD 1.65.
common_user_queries: Is soda water the same as sparkling water?
Is soda water good for high blood pressure?
Can I drink soda water on a diet?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain carbonated water (FDC ID 174831), which is an exact match for plain soda water. All core macros are confirmed zero by the approved source. GI convention applied per Project Veritas zero-calorie beverage rule.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as soda water contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no protein by formulation. fat_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no fat by formulation. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no digestible carbohydrates. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. All vitamin fields (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain carbonated water. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, iron_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas zero-calorie beverage convention. GL per 330 g serving = 0.0. Sodium 10 mg per 100 g is the USDA SR Legacy value for club soda / plain carbonated water; actual sodium may vary by brand. Food sourced from field survey data listing under Hock Kee Braised Beef Noodles 福记红烧牛肉面 - Lorong Telok at SGD 1.65. Multilingual names applied per Singaporean cuisine rules (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as soda water is a universally available beverage sold in Singapore.
